content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 3, 1987

Melvin Shapiro
3786 Alabama Street, B-306
San Diego, CA 92104

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-87-121

Dear Mr. Shapiro:

On April 17, 1987, we received your letter dated April 15, 1987. Unfortunately, it was misplaced. This is not a common occurrence in this agency, but it can happen. I hope you will accept my sincere apology for the delay in responding.

You have written with concerns about the San Diego Community College District Foundation. You believe that its directors should be filing statements of economic interest (SEIs), but they apparently have not done so.

The Political Reform Act 1/ requires the Commission to provide formal written advice to any person whose duties under the Act are in question. The Commission does not provide advice to third parties seeking advice concerning another person's duties under the Act. (Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B); copy enclosed) Consequently, we are not able to provide advice on the issue you raise in your letter.

Let me note, generally, that directors of community college auxiliary organizations are not required to file SEIs unless they are designated as filers by their agency's conflict of interest code. You have indicated that the San Diego Foundation has not so designated its directors. You might raise this issue with the foundation if you have not done so already.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:jaj

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Administrative Code Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Administrative Code.

428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

MELVIN SHAPIRO
3786 Alabama St. B-306
San Diego, CA 92104

April 15, 1987

FBr Political Practices Commission
PO Box 807
Sacramento,Ca 95804-0807

To Whom it may Concern:

The San Diego Community College District Foundation has not
filed any conflict of interest code nor has its directors
filed any statements of economic interest. I believe that the
Foundation is a public agency and should have filed.

The foundation was created by the San Diego Community College
District (District) and all of its directors are appointed by
the District. The Foundation performs functions that are identical
to the District, namely teaching of courses in the district's
classrooms. Furthermore, the Chancellor of the District is the
Chief Executive Officer of the Foundation. These are some of the
reasons that I believe the Foundation is a public agency and
should have filed statements with you.

I would appreciate receiving copies of any correspondence
between you and the Foundation plus your opinion on the
matter.

Sincerely,

Melvin Shapiro

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 21, 1987


Melvyn Shapiro
3786 Alabama Street, B-306
San Diego, Ca 92104

Re: 87-121

Dear Mr. Shapiro:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on April 17, 1987 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If your request is for informal assistance, we will
answer it as quickly as we can. (See Commission Regulation
18329 (2 Cal. Adm. Code Sec. 18329).) You also should be aware
that your letter and our response are public records which may
be disclosed to the public upon receipt of a proper request for
disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[illegible] signature

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

DMG:plh

sections.conclusion: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties seeking advice concerning another person's duties under the Act. Consequently, we are not able to provide advice on the issue you raise in your letter. Generally, directors of community college auxiliary organizations are not required to file SEIs unless they are designated as filers by their agency's conflict of interest code.
sections.question_synthetic: Should directors of the San Diego Community College District Foundation be filing statements of economic interest (SEIs)?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC cannot provide advice to third parties about another person's filing obligations, but notes that community college foundation directors are generally not required to file SEIs unless designated as filers by their agency's conflict of interest code.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains two related letters: acknowledgment letter (April 21, 1987) and substantive response letter (June 3, 1987). No formal QUESTION/CONCLUSION sections; extracted from letter body. FPPC declines advice based on Regulation 18329(b)(8)(B).

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Should directors of the San Diego Community College District Foundation be filing statements of economic interest (SEIs)?

CONCLUSION: The Commission does not provide advice to third parties seeking advice concerning another person's duties under the Act. Consequently, we are not able to provide advice on the issue you raise in your letter. Generally, directors of community college auxiliary organizations are not required to file SEIs unless they are designated as filers by their agency's conflict of interest code.

embedding.summary: The FPPC received an inquiry from a third party (Melvin Shapiro) about whether San Diego Community College District Foundation directors should file statements of economic interest. The FPPC declined to provide formal advice on third-party inquiries but provided general information that such directors are typically not required to file SEIs unless specifically designated.
